control.title: Scope-aware cargo build gate: include untracked files, validate package specs, and add a spec-declared gate contract
control.executiveSummary: spec-07 shipped only Bug A (dir→package-name resolution via cached `cargo metadata`); three remaining defect layers still break the gate: (B) `git diff --name-only` is blind to untracked files, so mandated e2e crates get dropped from scope; (C) the resolver's identity fallback emits invalid `-p <dir>` flags on name/asset drift; and (D) auto-deriving the test surface from a file diff is lossy for backend-wiring features, so the spec must declare its gate contract explicitly. This fix unions untracked files into the touched surface, hardens the resolver to NEVER emit an invalid `-p` (drop-on-mismatch, metadata-failure→workspace-wide), adds an optional `gate` contract (`packages`/`workspace`/`integration`) threaded from the specification stage through RunOptions into `runBuildGate` with documented precedence, and corrects the test suite that still encodes the original bug.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: Layer B — `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts ~L405) UNIONS two sources before extracting `crates/<seg>/` segments: tracked changes `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` AND new/untracked files `git -C <cwd> ls-files --others --exclude-standard`. Both stdouts are concatenated, then the existing `CRATE_SEGMENT_RE` runs over the union. Either command failing (non-git / bad ref / spawn error / non-zero) contributes nothing rather than throwing; an untracked-only change under `crates/workflows/` still yields `workflows` in the scope.

Layer C — `resolveCargoPackageNames` (~L327) DROPS unresolved dirs instead of the current per-element identity fallback: a resolved name is EMITTED only if it exists in the `cargo metadata` member map; an unresolved dir is discarded. On `cargo metadata` failure/missing/non-zero/timeout/bad-JSON (`loadCargoMetadata` returns `{ok:false}`) the function returns `[]` (NOT the touched dir names), so the gate widens to workspace-wide rather than emitting invalid `-p <dir>`. The whole-list identity fallback and per-element identity fallback are both removed. Never throws.

Layer C — a final validation gate inside `runBuildGate` (or the scoped-argv builder, e.g. `scopedCargoArgs`): BEFORE constructing any `-p <name>` flag, confirm each candidate name is a known `cargo metadata` member; drop any that aren't; if the surviving set is empty → workspace-wide. This uniform check applies to ALL sources (spec-declared, env, auto-detect). The opaque cargo error (`package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages … similar name: dtoa`) can no longer occur. Never throws.

Layer D — the specification-stage schema/model in src/render/schemas.ts gains an OPTIONAL `gate` field: `gate?: { packages?: string[]; workspace?: boolean; integration?: string[] }`. Backward compatible: when `gate` is omitted (non-backend/trivial specs, or repos with no contract), behavior is byte-identical to today. The field lives on the specification-stage OUTPUT (SpecificationData), not on RequirementsData.

Layer D — the specification-stage prompt builder (src/stages/*.ts) is updated to INSTRUCT the agent to declare `gate` for backend/integration features: name the cargo packages whose tests must pass, whether workspace-wide is required, and any e2e/integration target paths or binaries. For non-backend/trivial specs the prompt permits omitting `gate` (auto-detect applies).

Layer D — `RunOptions` (src/types.ts ~L243) gains `gate?: { packages?: string[]; workspace?: boolean; integration?: string[] }`. implementation.ts / verify.ts / index.ts read `state.spec?.gate` and pass it through `RunOptions.gate` into `runBuildGate` opts. Naming collision note: this is the RunOptions interface in src/types.ts, NOT the control-flow `gate` node referenced elsewhere.

Layer D — precedence in `runBuildGate` (HIGHEST→lowest): spec-declared `gate.packages` → `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` env → corrected auto-detect (`detectTouchedCargoPackages` with layers B+C) → workspace-wide. `gate.workspace === true` short-circuits to workspace-wide (ignores `packages`). `gate.integration` targets are APPENDED on top of whatever scope was chosen. When a declared `gate.packages` set is empty after validation (all unknown) → degrade to workspace-wide (consistent with AC-03).

Layer D — declared `gate.packages` are validated against the `cargo metadata` member map (same resolver); unknown names are DROPPED with a clear log line, never emitted as an invalid `-p`. `gate.integration` targets are appended to the test command (e.g. additional `cargo test --test <stem>` or path invocation) on top of the scoped packages.

Layer E — tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts is corrected: (1) mock `cargo metadata` to return a PREFIXED workspace (`crates/data`→`stockfan-data`, `crates/tools`→`stockfan-tools`, `crates/workflows`→`stockfan-workflows`) and assert `-p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows` (NOT `-p data`); (2) add an untracked-file case where the ONLY `crates/workflows/` change is an untracked `tests/e2e_*.rs` (via `git ls-files --others`) and `stockfan-workflows` still appears in scope; (3) add a drop-unresolved case (a touched dir resolving to NO member → dropped, not emitted; all-drop → workspace-wide argv); (4) add a new spec-declared-gate-contract test: `RunOptions.gate = { packages: ['stockfan-data','stockfan-workflows'], integration: ['crates/workflows/tests/e2e_x.rs'] }` → gate uses the validated names + appends the integration target, ignoring auto-detect.

End-to-end stockfan shape: touched dirs `data`/`tools`/`workflows` with an UNTRACKED e2e under `crates/workflows/tests/` resolves to `cargo build/test/clippy -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows` across ALL THREE captured argvs, AND the `workflows` crate is included (its inclusion comes from the untracked-file union in AC-01).
`npm run typecheck` passes (strict-clean); `npm test` passes after the corrections in AC-09 — existing-corrected assertions plus the new spec-declared-gate-contract test all green.

No regression: non-cargo repos, non-git dirs, and repos with NO `gate` contract produce output byte-identical to today (auto-detect → workspace-wide). The inscope/out-of-scope classification (`partitionErrorsByScope` / `isGreenForScope`) still works. The DEFAULT gate strategy stays auto-detect (only `gate.workspace===true` or a failed resolution widens to workspace); the default is NOT switched to workspace-wide.

nonFunctional: Performance: `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` remains cached per-absolute-cwd (existing cache at ~L171). The only new spawn is a single `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`, reusing the existing timeout envelope; no new runtime deps. The empty-input guard is preserved so metadata is spawned ONLY when there is something to resolve.

Security/Robustness: NEVER throw from the git helpers, the metadata resolver, the validator, or the gate. All git/metadata spawns use single discrete-argv `spawnSync` with no `shell:true`, so package/path data never reaches a shell. Any failure degrades to workspace-wide (drop invalid → widen), never to an invalid `-p`.

Backward compatibility: the change is confined to src/build-runner.ts (B+C), src/types.ts RunOptions + src/render/schemas.ts SpecificationData + spec prompt + the stage call sites (D), and tests (E). nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts/render templates/control-flow are NOT touched.

Theme method-binding constraint (must NOT regress): the real pi `Theme` is a CLASS — `fg(color,text){ return this.fgColors.get(color)... }`. Any prompt/render change that touches theme MUST call method-style (`theme.fg(...)`), NEVER destructure (`const fg = theme.fg; fg(...)` detaches from `this` and throws 'reading fgColors'). Guarded by tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts.
openQuestions: gate.integration entry format: the example shows a PATH ('crates/workflows/tests/e2e_screen_us_fallback.rs') but the prose says 'additional cargo test --test <stem> invocations'. Are integration entries PATHS (derive stem = basename minus '.rs', emit `cargo test --test <stem>` under the package scope) or pre-named test targets? Recommend: accept either — if an entry ends in '.rs', derive the stem via basename-without-extension; otherwise treat it as a named target. Confirm with the implementer before finalizing AC-08.

When declared `gate.packages` is non-empty but ALL fail metadata validation: should we fall through to env → auto-detect → workspace-wide, or short-circuit straight to workspace-wide? Recommend straight-to-workspace-wide (AC-03/AC-07 assume this) for consistency with 'never emit an invalid -p'. Confirm.

Defense-in-depth uniformity (AC-03): should the env var `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` also pass through the final metadata-validation gate (so a stale env name is dropped rather than emitted)? Recommend YES — validate every candidate name regardless of source. Confirm; if no, the env path bypasses the guard and the 'NEVER emit invalid -p' guarantee has a hole.

Where should the 'dropped unknown package' log line go — `console.warn`, or an existing structured logger convention in src/build-runner.ts? Minor, but confirm the repo's logging convention so AC-08's 'clear log line' is well-defined.

Confirm the gate contract is read from the PARSED specification-stage output (SpecificationData via STAGE_MODELS['specification']/STAGE_MODELS['spec'] at src/render/schemas.ts ~L233), not from the raw requirements markdown. The RequirementsData schema (~L73) does NOT carry a gate field — only the specification stage does.

control.title: Scope-aware cargo build-gate fix: git touched-surface union, metadata-validated `-p` emission, and a spec-declared gate contract
control.summary: The spec-07 fix shipped only Bug A (dir→package-name resolution). The three remaining layers are well-served by three established conventions. (B) "Touched surface" detection must UNION two git commands because `git diff --name-only` is invisible to untracked files: `git diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` (tracked, includes deletions/renames) + `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` (untracked, .gitignore-respecting). The codebase already wraps every git helper in try/catch returning `[]`, so adding the second command as a no-throw contributor is a 4-line append. (C) Defense-in-depth: `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` is the canonical machine-readable source of truth for workspace member names; the function already loads and caches it. The correct fix is to DROP unresolved dirs (never fall back to the dir name — that's exactly what produced the invalid `-p data` / "package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages … similar name: dtoa" error) and return `[]` on metadata failure so the gate widens to workspace-wide. (D) A spec-declared gate contract beats file-diff inference for backend-wiring features because acceptance criteria (workspace integration suite + an e2e) cannot be recovered from which files changed; an OPTIONAL `gate: { packages?, workspace?, integration? }` on SpecificationData with precedence spec→env→auto-detect→workspace-wide is the backward-compatible pattern. NOTE: external web corroboration was unavailable (firecrawl CLI not installed), so these are grounded in the codebase plus standard git/cargo conventions rather than freshly-fetched sources — flagged as an open issue.

options.name: Touched surface: union `git diff --merge-base` + `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` (recommended, matches AC-01)
options.tradeoffs: Captures both tracked changes (incl. deletions + rename-new-paths) AND untracked files that `git diff` misses — directly fixes the stockfan case where `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs` (untracked `??`) dropped `workflows` from scope. Both commands respect `.gitignore`/`.git/info/exclude` (`--exclude-standard`), so generated artifacts don't leak into scope. Tradeoff: does NOT capture staged-but-uncommitted files (`git diff --cached`); for a pre-commit gate that's fine since spec work is committed per-stage, but if the gate ever runs in an uncommitted working tree, staged wiring changes would be missed. Acceptable here — the gate runs post-implementation where changes are committed. Either command failing contributes nothing (the module already returns `[]` on non-zero/status/error), preserving the never-throw invariant. `--merge-base` requires git ≥2.30; acceptable for a dev tool. SRC-01: standard git porcelain semantics; git documentation of `ls-files --others --exclude-standard` and `diff --merge-base`.

options.name: Touched surface: single `git status --porcelain=v1` parse
options.tradeoffs: ONE spawn instead of two; `--porcelain` emits all states (modified, staged, untracked, deleted) in a uniform format. Tradeoff: it diffs working-tree+index against HEAD, NOT against an arbitrary base ref (`main`/`SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF`). For a feature-branch gate you specifically want changes vs the integration base, not vs the current branch tip, so `--porcelain` under-scopes (it would include only last-commit's changes if everything is committed) or over-scopes differently than `--merge-base`. The union-of-two-commands approach matches the existing `--merge-base` design and requires no refactor of the base-ref precedence already in `detectTouchedCargoPackages`. Reject: changes the semantics the gate depends on.

options.name: `-p` validation: drop-unresolved + metadata-failure→`[]` (recommended, matches AC-02/AC-03)
options.tradeoffs: Resolves each dir against the cached `cargo metadata` member map (already loaded by `loadCargoMetadata`); emits a name ONLY if it exists as a member, DROPS the dir otherwise. On metadata failure (`{ok:false}`) returns `[]` so the gate widens to workspace-wide instead of emitting invalid `-p <dir>`. This converts today's opaque cargo error into a safe silent widening and makes the failure mode monotone (scope only ever shrinks toward 'more thorough', never toward 'wrong package'). Tradeoff vs reactive try/catch: a real third-party package added to a touched dir but not yet in a freshly-cached metadata (stale cache) would be dropped — but the cache is per-run-per-cwd and `--no-deps` metadata is cheap/fast, so staleness within one run is not a concern. Adding a FINAL validation pass inside `runBuildGate`/`scopedCargoArgs` (validate every candidate name against members before emitting ANY `-p`, regardless of source) is true defense-in-depth: it protects against future spec/env drift too, not just auto-detect. BP-01: `cargo metadata` `packages[].name` is the canonical member-name source; `-p` accepts only real member names. SRC-02: cargo CLI metadata subcommand spec.

options.name: `-p` validation: reactive try/catch + retry workspace-wide
options.tradeoffs: Let cargo fail with the opaque error, catch the non-zero exit, then retry the subcommand workspace-wide. Simpler logic (no proactive validation), but: (1) produces noisy/cryptic stderr the first time, (2) wastes the spawn + its timeout budget per subcommand before recovering, (3) the retry doubles command count on every mis-scope, and (4) it's not truly defense-in-depth — it reacts rather than guarantees. The proactive drop approach catches the invalid name once (cheap, cached metadata) and the argv is correct on the first try. Reject the reactive-only approach; keep the `runBuildGate` non-zero-exit handling as a LAST line of defense but make proactive validation the primary guard.

options.name: Gate contract: optional `gate` on SpecificationData, threaded via `RunOptions.gate` (recommended, matches AC-04..AC-08)
options.tradeoffs: Declarative test-surface contract that the SPEC declares (which packages' tests must pass, whether workspace-wide is needed, any e2e/integration target) — this is the correct fix for 'backend server → run integration tests' because acceptance criteria are irrecoverable from a file diff. Optional + defaulted = byte-identical backward compat for non-backend/trivial specs and repos with no contract. Putting it on SpecificationData (the spec-stage OUTPUT) is right — it's a decision the spec owns, downstream stages consume. Precedence spec→env→auto-detect→workspace-wide gives operators an env override and a safe floor. `gate.workspace===true` short-circuit and `gate.integration` append are clean. Tradeoff vs a separate manifest file (`.super-dev/gate.json`): the schema field keeps the contract co-located with the spec it pertains to (single source of truth, versioned with the spec doc) rather than a loose file that can drift from the spec — better for an agent-driven pipeline. Tradeoff vs convention-based detection: convention (e.g. 'always also run any `tests/e2e_*`') is less expressive and still guesses; the explicit field lets the spec name exact package+integration targets. Naming-collision risk noted in AC-06: this `gate` is the RunOptions interface field, NOT the control-flow `gate` node — keep them visually/type distinct. BP-02: declarative acceptance contracts over heuristic file-diff inference (cargo-nextest filter expressions, CI matrices are the precedent for 'name your test surface explicitly').

options.name: Gate contract: switch DEFAULT strategy to workspace-wide always
options.tradeoffs: Simplest, fewest edge cases, never under-scopes — but explicitly OUT OF SCOPE per the task: it sacrifices the time savings of scoped gates (the whole point of spec-07) and the task forbids it ('Do NOT switch the DEFAULT gate strategy to workspace-wide'). Reject; keep auto-detect as the default fallback and only widen on `gate.workspace===true` or a failed resolution.

openIssues: External web search was unavailable in this environment (firecrawl CLI not installed; `npx firecrawl` could not resolve). The claims here are grounded in the codebase (src/build-runner.ts current state) and standard git/cargo CLI conventions rather than freshly-fetched sources. A maintainer may want to spot-check against current Cargo Book ('Cargo Targets' / `cargo metadata` JSON schema) and git docs (`git ls-files`, `git diff --merge-base`) before merging — the conventions are stable but a citation pass would harden BP-01/BP-02.

Staged-but-uncommitted files are NOT captured by the proposed union (`git diff --merge-base` + `git ls-files --others`). This is acceptable for the current gate (changes are committed per stage) but if the gate is ever run mid-implementation on an uncommitted working tree, a staged wiring change would be invisible. Decide explicitly whether to add `git diff --cached --name-only` (or document the committed-tree assumption).

`gate.integration` semantics need a concrete format decision before implementation: should entries be (a) test-binary stems run as `cargo test --test <stem>`, (b) raw file paths run as `cargo test <path>`, or (c) both (detect by pattern)? The spec says 'e.g. additional `cargo test --test <stem>` invocations OR passed as extra test binaries'. The implementation stage (AC-06) must pick one disambiguation rule; if a path has no matching test target, validate/drop it the same way as invalid package names (never emit a cargo error).

The metadata cache is keyed by absolute `cwd` and `--no-deps` is used — confirm the resolution reads ONLY workspace members (not path/path-dependency crates pulled in via `[patch]`/path deps). `--no-deps` returns workspace members + any local path dependencies declared in the workspace manifest; if a touched dir maps to a path-dependency rather than a workspace member, `matchPackageBySegment` could still resolve it, which is probably desired, but worth confirming the member map used for the FINAL validation pass (AC-03) treats path-dep names as valid `-p` targets. Cargo accepts `-p` for any package in the dependency graph reachable from the workspace, so path-dep names are valid `-p` — but a build/test of a path dep may not behave as expected for a 'gate'. Flag for the implementer to decide the validation set (workspace members only vs full graph).

control.title: Scope-aware Cargo Build Gate: touched-surface union, defense-in-depth validation, and a spec-declared gate contract
control.summary: All four blocking open issues from the prior attempt are resolved with freshly-fetched sources (this run reached git-scm.com and doc.rust-lang.org via curl; firecrawl is absent but direct web fetch worked). (1) Citation pass complete — git-diff man confirms `--merge-base <commit>` resolves to the merge base of commit & HEAD and `git diff <commit>` compares the WORKING TREE to that commit (so it captures staged files present on disk) [SRC-01]; git-ls-files man confirms `--others` = untracked and `--exclude-standard` honors .gitignore [SRC-02]; cargo-metadata man confirms `packages[]` under `--no-deps` = "workspace members only, don't fetch dependencies" (resolve is null) [SRC-03]; cargo-test man confirms `--test NAME` is target-scoped while the positional `name_filter` is a substring filter across all targets, NOT isolated [SRC-04]. (2) Staged-but-uncommitted files are already captured by `git diff --merge-base` for the common case (staged file is present in the working tree); the only blind spot is staged-then-worktree-reverted, which never occurs in this commit-per-stage pipeline, so we DOCUMENT the committed/worktree-present assumption rather than adding a third `git diff --cached` spawn (which would violate the "git ls-files --others is the only new process" constraint). (3) `gate.integration` entries are emitted as `cargo test --test <stem>` (target-scoped, canonical Cargo Book form), with the stem DERIVED deterministically from the declared value (basename, strip `.rs`/path), then VALIDATED by a filesystem stat of `<memberDir>/tests/<stem>.rs` before emitting — a miss is dropped with a log line (never emits cargo's `test target 'x' not found` error). (4) The `cargo metadata --no-deps` member map contains ONLY workspace members by spec, so the validation set is workspace-members-only by design; path-dependencies are intentionally dropped because building/testing an arbitrary graph node is not a meaningful gate target.

options.name: Touched-surface source set: union `git diff --merge-base` + `git ls-files --others` (RECOMMENDED)
options.tradeoffs: Two spawns. Covers committed-vs-base, worktree-present-staged changes, AND untracked files — the union closes the stockfan blind spot (untracked e2e under crates/workflows/). Matches the constraint that `git ls-files --others` is the only NEW process (diff --merge-base already exists in spec-07). Misses ONLY the staged-but-worktree-reverted case, which cannot occur in a commit-per-stage pipeline — documented as an explicit assumption rather than closed with a 3rd spawn. Either command failing contributes nothing (never throws). ALTERNATIVE considered: add `git diff --cached --merge-base <ref>` as a 3rd source — closes the theoretical gap but adds a spawn and contradicts the explicit process-count constraint; deferred to a documented future-hardening note if mid-implementation gating is ever needed.

options.name: Integration invocation: `cargo test --test <stem>`, stem derived from declared path, validated via fs stat (RECOMMENDED)
options.tradeoffs: `--test NAME` is target-scoped and canonical (SRC-04); deterministic and isolated. Stem derivation rule: declared value ending in `.rs` or containing a path separator -> basename minus `.rs`; bare token -> used as-is. Validation: `exists(join(memberDir,'tests',stem+'.rs'))` (filesystem stat, NOT a spawn) before emitting; miss -> drop with a log line so cargo's `error: test target 'x' not found` can never surface. Globs (`*?[]`) supported by cargo natively if ever needed. ALTERNATIVES rejected: (b) `cargo test <path>` positional — it's a substring NAME filter across ALL targets, not target-scoped, so it can accidentally run unrelated tests and does not isolate the target; (c) both-via-pattern-detection — more code paths and ambiguous edges with no real benefit since (a) subsumes the valid cases.

options.name: Validation set: workspace members only, from cached `cargo metadata --no-deps` (RECOMMENDED)
options.tradeoffs: SRC-03 confirms `packages[]` under `--no-deps` = workspace members only (`resolve` is null, path-deps/external deps excluded). Reuses the existing per-cwd cached spawn (no new process). A touched dir that resolves to a path-dependency rather than a workspace member returns no match -> DROPPED by Layer C, which is the correct gate semantics: building/testing an arbitrary reachable graph node is not a meaningful gate target. Cargo WOULD accept `-p <path-dep-name>`, but emitting it would be surprising and not what a gate intends, so workspace-members-only is the right, defensive choice. ALTERNATIVE: validate against the full graph (drop `--no-deps`) — heavier spawn, includes all transitive deps, and the broader set has no gate value; rejected.

options.name: Unresolved-dir handling: DROP and widen-to-workspace-wide on empty (RECOMMENDED) vs identity fallback
options.tradeoffs: The current identity fallback (`resolveCargoPackageNames` emits the dir name when no member matches) is the root cause of the stockfan failure — it produced invalid `-p data` that cargo rejected. Layer C replaces it: a resolved name is EMITTED only if present in the member map; unresolved dirs are discarded; metadata failure (`{ok:false}`) returns `[]` -> workspace-wide. `runBuildGate`/`scopedCargoArgs` re-validates every candidate from EVERY source (spec, env, auto-detect) before constructing `-p`, so future naming drift degrades silently to workspace-wide instead of throwing. Never throws from the resolver, validator, or gate (degrade: drop invalid -> workspace-wide). Backward-compatible: no-gate-contract / non-cargo / non-git repos produce byte-identical output to today.

control.title: Root-Cause Analysis: cargo build-gate emits invalid `-p <dir>` and drops untracked crates (Layers B–E)
control.summary:
The scope-aware cargo build gate (spec-07, already on `main`) fixed only the directory→package-name mapping for the prefix-as-name case (Bug A). Three remaining defects plus a self-encoding test suite keep the gate broken in the real shape that triggered this work (the `stockfan` workspace, where directories `data`/`tools`/`workflows` map to packages `stockfan-*`):

1. UNTRACKED BLINDNESS (Layer B): `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts:405) only spawns `git diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only`. `git diff` is invisible to untracked files (`??`). An untracked `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_screen_us_fallback.rs` therefore contributed nothing, so `workflows` (and its mandated e2e) was dropped from scope.

2. IDENTITY FALLBACK EMITS INVALID `-p` (Layer C): `resolveCargoPackageNames` (src/build-runner.ts:327) has TWO identity fallbacks that re-introduce the original bug — `!meta.ok` returns `dedupePreservingOrder(strDirs)` (whole-list identity, ~L343), and a per-element unmatched dir returns the dir NAME itself (`out.push(matched ? matched.name : d)`, ~L354). For `stockfan` this emits `-p data` (no such package), reproducing the opaque cargo error `package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages … similar name: dtoa`. There is also NO membership validation inside `runBuildGate` before names become `-p` flags.

3. NO SPEC-DECLARED GATE CONTRACT (Layer D): `SpecificationData` (src/render/schemas.ts:221) and `RunOptions` (src/types.ts:243) have no `gate` field. The test surface is auto-derived purely from a file diff, which is lossy for backend-wiring features (acceptance = workspace integration suite + an e2e cannot be inferred from changed files). Call sites implementation.ts:84 / index.ts:53 / verify.ts:87 never read `state.spec?.gate`.

4. TEST SUITE ENCODES THE BUG (Layer E): `tests/build-runner-autospace.test.ts` `routeSpawn` (L84–90) routes EVERY non-`git` command — including `cargo metadata` — to `{status:0, stdout:"", stderr:""}`. Empty stdout makes `loadCargoMetadata` return `{ok:false}` (L256), which triggers the whole-list identity fallback, so the resolver returns `["data"]`. The test then ASSERTS `-p data`, locking the bug in green.

The diagnosis and four-layer fix are already specified by the task ACs; this analysis confirms the exact code locations, the causal chain, and a deterministic reproduction. The single dominant root cause is the IDENTITY FALLBACK semantics: the resolver's failure mode is "guess the dir name" instead of "drop / widen to workspace-wide," and nothing downstream re-validates against the member map.
hypotheses: H1 (85%) — IDENTITY FALLBACK is the root: `resolveCargoPackageNames` returns directory NAMES on both whole-list failure (`!meta.ok` → `dedupePreservingOrder(strDirs)`, build-runner.ts:343) and per-element miss (`matched ? matched.name : d`, ~L354), and `runBuildGate` emits those names as `-p` flags with NO membership check (testPackages flows straight into scopedCargoArgs, runBuildGate ~L855). Prediction: for a prefixed workspace the gate emits `-p data` and cargo rejects it with 'did not match any packages … similar name: dtoa'. FALSIFIABLE: if a member-map validation gate existed before argv construction, no invalid `-p` could ever be emitted regardless of resolver output.

H2 (90%) — UNTRACKED BLINDNESS independently drops the workflows crate: `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (build-runner.ts:405) spawns ONLY `git diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` (~L419). Prediction: a worktree whose ONLY change under `crates/workflows/` is an untracked file produces an empty `git diff` stdout, so `workflows` never enters the touched set even though `git status` shows `?? crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs`. FALSIFIABLE: unioning `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` would surface the untracked path and the segment regex would extract `workflows`.

H3 (88%) — TEST MOCK ACCIDENTALLY ENCODES THE BUG: `routeSpawn` (build-runner-autoscope.test.ts:84) returns `{status:0, stdout:''}` for all non-`git` commands, so the mocked `cargo metadata` call yields `{ok:false}` and the whole-list identity fallback fires — the test's `-p data` assertion only passes BECAUSE it exercises the failure path. Prediction: correcting the resolver to drop unresolved names would make these assertions fail without test updates (Layer E). FALSIFIABLE: a prefixed-metadata fixture (`crates/data`→`stockfan-data`) would require asserting `-p stockfan-data`.

H4 (70%) — SECONDARY: even with H1/H2 fixed, auto-detect is structurally lossy for backend-wiring features whose acceptance criteria (workspace integration suite + an e2e) cannot be derived from a file diff. This is what H1/H2 paper over but don't truly fix; the spec-declared `gate` contract (Layer D) is the real remediation. Prediction: any backend feature needing `cargo test --test <e2e>` in addition to scoped packages will be under-tested without an explicit contract.

H5 (low, rejected as the cause) — theme/method-binding regression detaches `this`. Rejected: no Theme rendering is on this code path; tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts guards it and the bug reproduces without any theme involvement.
control.rootCause:
VERIFIED ROOT CAUSE — the resolver's failure semantics are "guess the directory name" rather than "drop / widen to workspace-wide," and the gate never re-validates emitted names against the cargo member map. Three concrete, independently-confirmable defects, each pinned to exact code:

1. IDENTITY FALLBACK (dominant, build-runner.ts:327 `resolveCargoPackageNames`):
   - whole-list fallback: `if (!meta.ok) return dedupePreservingOrder(strDirs);` (~L343) returns directory NAMES verbatim on any metadata failure;
   - per-element fallback: `out.push(matched ? matched.name : d);` (~L354) returns the directory NAME for an unmatched segment.
   Both reproduce `-p data` for `stockfan`. The fix (Layer C) replaces both: return `[]` on `!meta.ok`, and DROP unmatched segments instead of pushing `d`.

2. NO VALIDATION GATE (build-runner.ts `runBuildGate` ~L825–855): `testPackages` from any tier flows straight into `scopedCargoArgs`/`scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoTestArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs` with no membership check. The fix adds a validation pass before argv construction: confirm each name is a known member, drop unknowns, and widen to workspace-wide if the surviving set is empty.

3. UNTRACKED BLINDNESS (build-runner.ts:405 `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, ~L419): only `git diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` is spawned; `git diff` does not see untracked files. The fix (Layer B) concatenates `git -C <cwd> ls-files --others --exclude-standard` and runs the existing `CRATE_SEGMENT_RE` over the union; either command failing contributes nothing (never throws).

CONFIRMING EVIDENCE the test green-lit the bug: `routeSpawn` (tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts:84–90) returns `{status:0, stdout:''}` for every non-`git` command, so the mocked `cargo metadata` produces empty stdout → `loadCargoMetadata` returns `{ok:false}` (build-runner.ts:256, the `out === ""` branch) → whole-list identity fallback → `["data"]` → `-p data`. The test asserts exactly `-p data`, encoding the defect. Layer E replaces this with a prefixed-metadata fixture asserting `-p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows` plus untracked-file, drop-unresolved, and spec-declared-contract cases.

RECOMMENDED FIX: apply Layers B+C to src/build-runner.ts (union untracked files; drop unresolved; metadata-failure→[]; add the membership validation gate before argv construction); add the optional `gate?: { packages?; workspace?; integration? }` to SpecificationData (src/render/schemas.ts:221) and RunOptions (src/types.ts:243); instruct the spec prompt to declare `gate` for backend features; thread `state.spec?.gate` through implementation.ts:84 / index.ts:53 / verify.ts:87 into `runBuildGate`; implement precedence spec→env→auto-detect→workspace-wide with `gate.workspace` short-circuit and `gate.integration` appended; correct tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts (Layer E). All four fixes degrade silently (never throw) and are byte-identical for non-cargo / non-git / no-contract repos. REGRESSION TEST: the new autoscope cases (prefixed fixture, untracked-only workflows change, all-unresolved→workspace-wide, and the spec-declared contract test) plus `npm run typecheck` strict-clean.
reproductionSteps: REPRO 1 (deterministic, hermetic — the bug the test currently asserts): run `npx vitest run tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts` SCENARIO-006. The harness mocks `git diff` → `crates/data/src/lib.rs` and ALL other commands (incl. `cargo metadata`) → `{status:0,stdout:''}`. Empty stdout forces `loadCargoMetadata` to `{ok:false}` (build-runner.ts:256), the whole-list identity fallback returns `['data']`, and the gate emits `cargo build/test/clippy -p data`. OBSERVE: test PASSES asserting `-p data` — that pass IS the bug being green-lit.

REPRO 2 (real opaque cargo error — the field symptom): in a real prefixed workspace where `crates/data` → package `stockfan-data`, run the gate over a touched `crates/data/src/lib.rs`. `detectTouchedCargoPackages` extracts `data`; `resolveCargoPackageNames`' per-element fallback emits `data` (no member match); `cargo build -p data` exits non-zero with `error: package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages; did you mean 'dtoa'?`. OBSERVE: gate fails on a valid, in-scope change.

REPRO 3 (untracked blindness — the stockfan e2e drop): create a worktree whose ONLY change under `crates/workflows/` is an untracked `tests/e2e_screen_us_fallback.rs` (`git status` → `??`). Run the gate. `git diff --merge-base main --name-only` returns empty for that path (diffs never list `??` files), so `workflows` never enters the touched set and `stockfan-workflows` (incl. its e2e) is skipped. CONFIRM via `git diff` (empty) vs `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` (lists the untracked path) — the union in the fix is what makes `workflows` reappear.

REPRO 4 (end-to-end stockfan shape, post-fix acceptance): touched dirs data/tools/workflows WITH an untracked e2e under crates/workflows/tests/, against a prefixed-metadata fixture. EXPECT all three captured argvs to read `cargo build/test/clippy -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows --quiet`, with `workflows` included via the untracked union, and a spec-declared `RunOptions.gate={packages:['stockfan-data','stockfan-workflows'], integration:['crates/workflows/tests/e2e_x.rs']}` to validate those names + append the integration target while ignoring auto-detect.

VERIFICATION: `npm run typecheck` (strict-clean) and `npm test` (existing-corrected + new) green; non-cargo / non-git / no-gate-contract runs remain byte-identical (auto-detect→workspace-wide); inscope/out-of-scope classification (`partitionErrorsByScope`/`isGreenForScope`) unaffected.

control.title: Codebase Assessment: scope-aware cargo build-gate fix (Layers B–E)
control.summary: pi-super-dev is a strict-TS pi-extension (a control-flow pipeline library, NOT a runnable server) whose build/test/typecheck gate lives in `src/build-runner.ts`. Spec-07 already shipped `resolveCargoPackageNames` + `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (cached `cargo metadata`, git-diff crate extraction). The remaining work (B: untracked-file union, C: drop-invalid defense-in-depth, D: spec-declared `gate` contract, E: test corrections) must mirror three load-bearing conventions: (1) every git/cargo helper is wrapped in try/catch and degrades to `[]`/`{ok:false}` — NEVER throws; (2) argv are always discrete-element `spawnSync` arrays with no `shell:true`; (3) stage output schemas are typebox `Type.Object({...})` models in `src/render/schemas.ts`, state fields are typed `ControlObj` in `PipelineState`, and `RunOptions` is the single threading surface into `runBuildGate`. There is NO API or UI server — verification is `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) + `npm test` (vitest run). Layer D's new `gate` field adds a FIFTH (top) precedence tier to the existing four-tier chain in `runBuildGate`. Layer E's test file (`tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts`) currently encodes the original bug (`-p data` on a dir==name mock) and mocks `spawnSync` per-call — it must grow a `cargo metadata` JSON route and an `ls-files --others` git route.

patterns.name: Never-throw degradation (side-effecting helpers → `[]` / `{ok:false}`)
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts: detectTouchedCargoPackages try/catch → return [] ; loadCargoMetadata → cache+return {ok:false} on any error/timeout/empty/bad-JSON
patterns.consistency: Strict & pervasive. Every git/cargo spawn in build-runner.ts is fully try/caught and degrades silently. Layer B's new `git ls-files` route, Layer C's resolver change (drop-unresolved, metadata-fail → []), and the new final validator MUST follow this exactly — the acceptance criteria forbid throwing from any of them.

patterns.name: Discrete-argv spawnSync, never `shell:true`
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts loadCargoMetadata: spawnSync("cargo", ["metadata","--format-version","1","--no-deps","--manifest-path", join(absCwd,"Cargo.toml")], {encoding:"utf8", timeout:...}) — package/path data never reaches a shell
patterns.consistency: Universal in this module. Layer B's `git -C <cwd> ls-files --others --exclude-standard` and any `cargo test --test <stem>` integration invocations (Layer D) must be discrete-element argv arrays with `{encoding:"utf8"}`.

patterns.name: Module-level cached metadata, keyed by `resolve(cwd)`
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts: const cargoMetadataCache = new Map<string,CargoMetadataResult>(); loadCargoMetadata keys on absCwd = resolve(cwd); SCENARIO-018 caches failures to avoid re-spawn
patterns.consistency: Layer C's validation and Layer D's declared-package validation should reuse `loadCargoMetadata(cwd)` (it is NOT exported — validation must live inside build-runner.ts or be a new private helper sharing that cache), NOT spawn cargo again. Per-cwd caching is the established contract.

patterns.name: Typebox stage schemas + `Type.Optional(...)` for optional fields
patterns.example: src/render/schemas.ts:221 SpecificationData = Type.Object({...}); CodeAssessmentData uses services: Type.Optional(Type.Object({...})) and openQuestions: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String()))
patterns.consistency: Layer D: add `gate: Type.Optional(Type.Object({ packages: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String())), workspace: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean()), integration: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String())) }))` to SpecificationData, mirroring the existing `services`/`openQuestions` optional-object/array precedent.

patterns.name: Precedence-chain JSDoc + if/else-if implementation in `runBuildGate`
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts runBuildGate: opts.testPackages !== undefined → env SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES → detectTouchedCargoPackages (rust only) → [] ; each tier documented in the module-level JSDoc at top of file
patterns.consistency: Layer D inserts spec-declared `gate.packages` as a NEW TOP tier (spec → env → auto-detect → workspace-wide). `gate.workspace===true` must short-circuit before tiers below. Keep the same if/else-if shape and extend the JSDoc block.

patterns.name: RunOptions is the single call-site threading surface; call sites pass only `{signal}`
patterns.consistency: Layer D: add `gate?: {packages?;workspace?;integration?}` to RunOptions in src/types.ts, then read `state.spec?.gate` (note spec is `ControlObj` — read defensively) at all three call sites and pass it through. Keep `{signal}` intact alongside it.

patterns.name: Spec prompt builder lives behind the `P.*` prompt module, wired from writers.ts
patterns.consistency: Layer D: the instruction telling the agent to declare `gate` for backend features is added in the `buildSpecPrompt` builder (the `P` module), NOT in writers.ts itself. writers.ts only composes args.

patterns.name: Test harness: mock `node:child_process.spawnSync`, real temp `Cargo.toml`, save/restore env
patterns.example: tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts: vi.mock("node:child_process"); routeSpawn() routes git→stdout, cargo→capture+succeed; rustTmp() writes a real Cargo.toml; withEnv() saves/restores SUPER_DEV_* vars
patterns.consistency: Layer E MUST extend routeSpawn to (a) route a `cargo metadata` call → return prefixed-workspace JSON, and (b) route a second git argv (`ls-files --others`) for the untracked case. Existing helper style (cargoCalls ref, cargoArgvFor, callsFor) should be reused, not replaced.

recommendations: Mirror the existing git helper exactly for Layer B: add `spawnSync("git", ["-C", cwd, "ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard"], {encoding:"utf8"})` in `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, CONCATENATE its stdout with the `--merge-base` stdout, then run the same `CRATE_SEGMENT_RE` over the union. Keep the try/catch→`[]` contract so either git call failing contributes nothing. Update SCENARIO-001/020 prose in JSDoc to mention the untracked union.

Layer C: (a) change `resolveCargoPackageNames` so an unmatched segment is DROPPED (push nothing) instead of pushing `d`; (b) change the metadata-`{ok:false}` branch to `return []` (NOT identity fallback) so the gate widens to workspace-wide; (c) add a private `validatePackageNames(cwd, names)` reusing `loadCargoMetadata` and call it inside `runBuildGate` before building `-p` flags — drop unknowns with a single ctx.log/console line, never throw. This converts the opaque cargo error into a silent widening.

Layer D: add `gate` to `SpecificationData` (typebox Optional, mirroring `services`/`openQuestions`), read it in verify.ts/index.ts/implementation.ts as `state.spec?.gate` (defensive — spec is `ControlObj`), thread via a new optional `gate?` on `RunOptions`, and insert it as the NEW TOP precedence tier above `opts.testPackages`. Precedence: `gate.workspace===true` → workspace-wide (short-circuit); else `gate.packages` (validated, dropped if unknown) → `opts.testPackages`/`SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` → `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (B+C) → `[]`. `gate.integration` targets append extra `cargo test --test <stem>` exec calls inside the existing `exec` loop.

Layer E: rewrite build-runner-autoscope.test.ts fixtures to a prefixed workspace (`crates/data`→`stockfan-data`, `crates/tools`→`stockfan-tools`, `crates/workflows`→`stockfan-workflows`) by routing a `cargo metadata` call in routeSpawn to return JSON `{packages:[{name:"stockfan-data",manifest_path:"<tmp>/crates/data/Cargo.toml"},...]}`. Assert `-p stockfan-*` (NOT `-p data`). Add: (1) untracked case where git `ls-files --others` returns `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs`; (2) drop-unresolved case (a touched dir with no member → not emitted; all-drop → workspace-wide argv); (3) new spec-gate test passing `gate:{packages:["stockfan-data","stockfan-workflows"], integration:["crates/workflows/tests/e2e_x.rs"]}` via RunOptions → validated names + appended `cargo test --test e2e_x` (or path), ignoring auto-detect and spawning no git. control.title: Scope-aware Cargo Build Gate: Untracked Inclusion, Defense-in-Depth Validation, and Spec-Declared Gate Contract
control.summary: Complete the scope-aware cargo build-gate fix that spec-07 (already merged, DO NOT redo) started. Spec-07 shipped only Bug A — resolving touched crate dirs to package names via cached `cargo metadata`. This task delivers the three remaining defect layers the diagnosis requires plus a test correction: (B) include UNTRACKED files in the touched surface by unioning `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` with `git diff --merge-base` so mandated e2e crates are never silently dropped; (C) defense-in-depth validation that NEVER emits an invalid `-p` — the resolver DROPS unresolved dirs (removing the current per-element + whole-list identity fallbacks that produced the invalid `-p data` which crashed the stockfan gate), metadata failure returns `[]` to widen safely to workspace-wide, and a final validator re-checks every candidate name against the member map before any `-p` flag is built regardless of source; (D) an OPTIONAL spec-declared `gate` contract `{ packages?, workspace?, integration? }` that lets the specification stage declare its exact test surface for backend/integration features instead of the framework lossily inferring it from a file diff, threaded from SpecificationData through RunOptions into `runBuildGate` as a new top precedence tier; and (E) correction of the test suite that still encodes the original dir==name bug. All four layers preserve the never-throw degrade-to-workspace-wide invariant, use discrete-argv `spawnSync` (no shell), add no new runtime deps, and are byte-identical to today for non-cargo / non-git / no-gate-contract repos. Verified by `npm run typecheck` (strict-clean) and `npm test` (vitest, all green).
control.architecture:
The deterministic build gate lives in `src/build-runner.ts` and is consumed by three call sites — `src/stages/verify.ts:87`, `src/stages/index.ts:53`, `src/stages/implementation.ts:84` — each importing `runBuildGate` (aliased as `n`) and currently calling it as `n(setup.worktreePath, { signal: ctx.signal })`. The gate resolves a scoped cargo package set, then runs `cargo build/test/clippy -p <name>` per surviving name, or workspace-wide when the set is empty. This change layers four fixes onto that single module plus the specification schema/types/prompt, without touching nodes.ts, workflow.ts, pipeline.ts, render templates, or the control-flow layer.

LAYER B — untracked union (AC-01): `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd, baseRef?)` at build-runner.ts:405 currently runs only `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only`. It gains a second discrete-argv spawn — `spawnSync("git", ["-C", cwd, "ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard"], {encoding:"utf8"})` — and CONCATENATES both stdouts before the existing module-level `CRATE_SEGMENT_RE` (`/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//`) runs over the union. Either git command failing (non-git dir, unresolvable ref, spawn error, non-zero exit) contributes nothing rather than throwing, so the union degrades gracefully. This is the ONLY new spawned process; it reuses the existing timeout envelope and adds no runtime deps. Per-cwd caching of `cargo metadata` is unchanged (SCENARIO-037).

LAYER C — defense-in-depth validation (AC-02, AC-03): `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` at build-runner.ts:327 currently has TWO identity fallbacks that together caused the stockfan crash: a whole-list fallback `return dedupePreservingOrder(strDirs)` when `loadCargoMetadata` returns `{ok:false}`, and a per-element fallback `out.push(matched ? matched.name : d)`. Both are removed. New semantics: when metadata is unavailable the function returns `[]` (so the gate widens to workspace-wide, never guessing names); in the per-element loop, a dir that does not match any `cargo metadata --no-deps` member is DROPPED (push nothing). This converts the opaque cargo error (`package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages … similar name: dtoa`) into a silent widening. Additionally, a private `validatePackageNames(cwd, names)` helper reuses the cached `loadCargoMetadata` to return the subset of candidate names that are known workspace members; `runBuildGate` (or its scoped-argv builder) routes EVERY chosen candidate set — regardless of source — through this validator before constructing any `-p <name>` flag, dropping unknowns with a single log line and widening to workspace-wide if the surviving set is empty. The validation set is workspace-members-only by design (`--no-deps`; path-dependencies are intentionally not gate targets). Never throws from the resolver, validator, or gate.

LAYER D — spec-declared gate contract (AC-04 through AC-08): The specification-stage OUTPUT schema `SpecificationData` in `src/render/schemas.ts:221` gains an OPTIONAL `gate: Type.Optional(Type.Object({ packages: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String())), workspace: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean()), integration: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String())) }))`, mirroring the existing `services`/`openQuestions` optional-object precedent. When omitted (non-backend/trivial specs, or repos with no contract) behavior is byte-identical to today. The specification prompt builder `P.buildSpecPrompt` (composed at `src/stages/writers.ts:58`) is extended to INSTRUCT the agent to declare `gate` for backend/integration features — naming the cargo packages whose tests must pass, whether workspace-wide is required, and any e2e/integration target paths — while permitting omission for trivial specs. `RunOptions` in `src/types.ts:243` gains `gate?: { packages?: string[]; workspace?: boolean; integration?: string[] }` (note: this is the RunOptions interface, NOT the control-flow `gate` node). The three call sites read `state.spec?.gate` defensively (spec is a `ControlObj`) and pass it through `RunOptions.gate` alongside the existing `{signal}`. Precedence in `runBuildGate` becomes, HIGHEST→lowest: (1) `gate.workspace === true` → workspace-wide SHORT-CIRCUIT (ignores packages); (2) spec-declared `gate.packages` → validated; (3) `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` env → validated; (4) corrected auto-detect (`detectTouchedCargoPackages` with layers B+C); (5) `[]` → workspace-wide. When any declared package set is empty after validation (all unknown) → degrade to workspace-wide. `gate.integration` targets are APPENDED on top of whatever scope was chosen: a target ending in `.rs` or containing a path separator derives its stem (basename minus `.rs`) and emits `cargo test --test <stem>` under the scoped packages, validated by a filesystem stat of `<memberDir>/tests/<stem>.rs` (no new spawn) with misses dropped and logged; a bare token is used as-is. This never surfaces cargo's `test target 'x' not found` error.

LAYER E — test correction (AC-09, AC-10): `tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts` currently encodes the original bug (asserting `-p data` on a dir==name mock). Its `routeSpawn` router is extended to (a) route a `cargo metadata` call to a prefixed-workspace JSON fixture (`crates/data`→`stockfan-data`, `crates/tools`→`stockfan-tools`, `crates/workflows`→`stockfan-workflows`) and (b) distinguish `git diff` from `git ls-files` by argv inspection. Existing assertions are corrected to expect `-p stockfan-*`. New cases: an untracked-only `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs` change (via `ls-files --others`) still yields `stockfan-workflows`; a touched dir resolving to no member is dropped (all-drop → workspace-wide argv); and a spec-declared `RunOptions.gate = { packages: ["stockfan-data","stockfan-workflows"], integration: ["crates/workflows/tests/e2e_x.rs"] }` drives validated names + appended `cargo test --test e2e_x`, ignoring auto-detect and spawning no git.

CRITICAL INVARIANTS: never throw from git helpers, metadata resolver, validator, or gate — always degrade (drop invalid → widen to workspace-wide). All spawns are discrete-argv `spawnSync` arrays with `{encoding:"utf8"}`, no `shell:true`, so package/path data never reaches a shell. `cargo metadata --no-deps` stays cached per-absolute-cwd (existing `cargoMetadataCache` Map); the empty-input guard is preserved so metadata spawns only when there is something to resolve. The pi `Theme` is a CLASS — any prompt/render change touching theme MUST call method-style (`theme.fg(...)`), never destructure; guarded by `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts`.
control.testingStrategy:
Verification is `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit, strict-clean) and `npm test` (vitest run); there is no API or UI server. The primary unit-test surface is `tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts`, which mocks `node:child_process.spawnSync` via `vi.mock` and routes calls through a `routeSpawn` router. The router is extended to: (1) route a `cargo metadata` invocation to a prefixed-workspace JSON fixture with `packages:[{name:"stockfan-data",manifest_path:"<tmp>/crates/data/Cargo.toml"}, ...]` and real temp `Cargo.toml` files written by the existing `rustTmp()` helper; (2) distinguish `git diff --merge-base` from `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` by argv inspection rather than just `cmd==="git"`; and (3) capture cargo build/test/clippy argvs via the existing `cargoCalls`/`cargoArgvFor`/`callsFor` helpers. Env vars are saved/restored via the existing `withEnv()` helper.

Corrected & new test cases: (a) the existing assertions are flipped to expect `cargo build/test/clippy -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows` (NOT `-p data`); (b) an untracked-only case where `ls-files --others` returns `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_screen_us_fallback.rs` still surfaces `stockfan-workflows`; (c) a drop-unresolved case where a touched dir resolves to no member is omitted from `-p` and all-drop collapses to a workspace-wide argv; (d) a spec-declared-gate-contract test passing `RunOptions.gate = {packages:["stockfan-data","stockfan-workflows"], integration:["crates/workflows/tests/e2e_x.rs"]}` produces validated `-p` names plus an appended `cargo test --test e2e_x`, ignoring auto-detect and spawning no git; (e) precedence assertions covering spec>env>auto and a `gate.workspace===true` short-circuit; (f) an end-to-end stockfan-shape test (AC-10) confirming all three captured argvs carry the three prefixed packages with the workflows crate included via the untracked union.

Regression & guard testing: non-cargo repos, non-git directories, and repos with no `gate` contract must produce output byte-identical to today (the DEFAULT gate strategy stays auto-detect; only `gate.workspace===true` or a failed resolution widens to workspace). The scope-classification helpers (`partitionErrorsByScope` / `isGreenForScope`) must continue partitioning correctly. The theme method-binding regression test `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` must remain green. Defense-in-depth never-throw behavior is asserted by forcing metadata-fail and unknown-name inputs and confirming `[]`/workspace-wide output with no thrown error. Spawn hygiene is asserted by confirming argv are discrete arrays and that metadata is not spawned on empty input. Performance invariants: metadata cached per-cwd (single spawn across repeated resolutions) and exactly one new `ls-files` spawn reusing the existing timeout envelope. Acceptance is met when `npm run typecheck` is strict-clean and `npm test` is fully green (existing-corrected + new tests).

result.text:
I have enough grounding to render the verdict. Key verification results:

**Grounded (verified against codebase):** `resolveCargoPackageNames`@build-runner.ts:327 (with both identity fallbacks at L342/L347 ✓), `detectTouchedCargoPackages`@:405 (merge-base only ✓), `runBuildGate`@:810, `CRATE_SEGMENT_RE`@:158, `loadCargoMetadata`/`cargoMetadataCache`, `matchPackageBySegment`, all 3 call sites (verify.ts:87, index.ts:53, implementation.ts:84), `P.buildSpecPrompt`@writers.ts:58, `SpecificationData`/`RunOptions`/`services`+`openQuestions` precedent, `scopedCargo*Args`, `parseTestPackages`, theme test file, and `validatePackageNames` correctly absent (new).

**Defects found:**
- Architecture section cites `tests/build-runner-autospace.test.ts` ("autospace") — actual file is `build-runner-autoscope.test.ts` ("autoscope"). The Testing Strategy section uses the correct name → internal inconsistency + grounding miss.
- `partitionErrorsByScope` / `isGreenForScope` referenced in Testing Strategy — grep across `src/`+`tests/` finds **neither exists** anywhere.
- Test-correction scope names only one file; 5+ sibling build-runner test files may encode the same dir==name bug.

control.title: Spec Review — Scope-aware Cargo Build Gate (08-cargo-build-gate-validation)
control.summary: The specification is architecturally sound, strongly grounded in the actual build-runner.ts module, and traces all 12 ACs to four well-scoped layers (B: untracked union, C: defense-in-depth validation, D: spec-declared gate contract, E: test correction). The core implementation targets are verified: resolveCargoPackageNames@build-runner.ts:327 genuinely contains the two identity fallbacks the spec removes (whole-list @L342, per-element @L347); detectTouchedCargoPackages@:405 runs only `git diff --merge-base`; runBuildGate@:810 has the documented four-tier precedence; all three call sites (verify.ts:87, index.ts:53, implementation.ts:84) and P.buildSpecPrompt@writers.ts:58 are accurate; SpecificationData/RunOptions/services+openQuestions precedents and the scoped-argv helpers all exist; validatePackageNames is correctly absent (new). Grounding score is ~87% (20/23 references verified), which triggers a HIGH finding. Two HIGH grounding defects block a clean APPROVED: (1) the Architecture section names a non-existent test file `build-runner-autospace.test.ts` (actual: `build-runner-autoscope.test.ts`), creating an internal inconsistency with the Testing Strategy; and (2) `partitionErrorsByScope`/`isGreenForScope` are cited as must-stay-green helpers but neither exists anywhere in src/ or tests/. Additional MEDIUM gaps: test-correction scope names only one of ~6 sibling build-runner test files that could encode the same dir==name bug, and the integration-target validator leaves `memberDir` selection ambiguous under multi-package scope. No Critical defects; ACs appear fully covered; verdict APPROVED WITH REVISIONS pending the grounding fixes.

findings.title: Architecture section names a non-existent test file (autospace vs autoscope)
findings.detail: The Architecture / LAYER E paragraph references `tests/build-runner-autospace.test.ts` ('autospace', with an 'n'), but the actual file on disk is `tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts` ('autoscope', with a 'c'). The Testing Strategy section uses the correct 'autoscope' spelling, so the spec is internally inconsistent AND grounds to a file an implementer will not find via literal path. Recommendation: correct the Architecture reference to `tests/build-runner-autoscope.test.ts` everywhere.

findings.title: Hallucinated scope-classification helpers partitionErrorsByScope / isGreenForScope
findings.detail: The Testing Strategy states: 'The scope-classification helpers (`partitionErrorsByScope` / `isGreenForScope`) must continue partitioning correctly.' A grep across both `src/` and `tests/` returns ZERO matches for either symbol. The repo does have `tests/build-runner-inscope-classification.test.ts`, but neither named function exists. An implementer cannot satisfy AC-11 ('confirm partitionErrorsByScope/isGreenForScope still work') against functions that do not exist, and the in-scope verdict comment in implementation.ts:85 references AC-05/SCENARIO-012-014 — suggesting the real helpers may be named differently (or are inline). Recommendation: either cite the actual helper names (verify via grep) or drop this clause and replace with the real in-scope classification surface the gate produces.

findings.title: Test-correction scope names only one file; sibling build-runner tests may encode the same bug
findings.detail: LAYER E/AC-09/AC-10 scope all corrections to `build-runner-autoscope.test.ts`, but the repo contains at least five sibling files that plausibly assert on the same dir==name assumption or `-p data`-style argv: `build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts`, `build-runner-packages.test.ts`, `build-runner-package-wiring.test.ts`, `build-runner-scoped-args.test.ts`, and `build-runner-touched-crates.test.ts`. If any of these assert on the old identity fallback (which LAYER C removes) or on pre-union touched-set behavior (which LAYER B changes), the 'npm test fully green' AC-12 acceptance will fail. Recommendation: enumerate which sibling test files are explicitly in-scope for correction vs. guaranteed-unchanged, or add an AC asserting no sibling test encodes the dir==name identity fallback.

findings.title: Integration-target validation leaves memberDir selection ambiguous under multi-package scope
findings.detail: LAYER D specifies that a `.rs`-suffixed or path-bearing integration target derives its stem and is validated by a filesystem stat of `<memberDir>/tests/<stem>.rs`. When the scoped package set contains MORE than one member (the common case — e.g. {stockfan-data, stockfan-workflows}), it is unspecified which `memberDir` is stat'd: the first surviving package, the union across all, or a per-target declared package. This ambiguity can cause a target to be silently dropped (miss stat) when the test file lives under a different member than the one chosen. Recommendation: state the selection rule explicitly (e.g. 'union: stat succeeds if ANY scoped member's tests/<stem>.rs exists') and add a SCENARIO covering a multi-package scope with a cross-member integration target.

findings.title: Minor line-number drift in two citations
findings.detail: SpecificationData is cited at `src/render/schemas.ts:221` but the `export const SpecificationData` declaration begins around line 224; RunOptions is cited at `src/types.ts:243` but `export interface RunOptions` is at line 242. Both are off by 1-3 lines. Low impact (nearby), but for a spec that elsewhere cites exact anchors used as implementation targets, this reduces grounding precision. Recommendation: re-anchor to the exact declaration lines.

BUT: (1) Layer B — the `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` union — does not exist anywhere; `detectTouchedCargoPackages` still runs only `git diff --merge-base --name-only`, so the stockfan untracked e2e (`crates/workflows/tests/e2e_screen_us_fallback.rs`) is STILL dropped from scope, leaving the build gate blind to exactly the regression it was created to catch. (2) Layer D — the spec-declared `gate: { packages?, workspace?, integration? }` contract — is absent from `src/types.ts` (RunOptions has no `gate` field), `src/render/schemas.ts` (SpecificationData unchanged), all `src/stages/*.ts`, and `runBuildGate`'s precedence tier (still just opts→env→auto-detect→workspace, with no spec-gate tier). findings.title: Layer B (untracked-file union) is not implemented — the motivating bug persists

The fix requires unioning `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` with `git -C <cwd> ls-files --others --exclude-standard` before extracting `crates/<seg>/`. grep across src/ + tests/ for `ls-files|--others|exclude-standard` returns ZERO matches. `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts:467-485) still spawns only `git diff --merge-base ref --name-only`. Consequence: an untracked-only change under `crates/workflows/` yields an empty scope → workspace-wide or skipped → AC-01 and AC-06 unmet; the stockfan regression is NOT fixed. This is a production-correctness failure: the 'HARD test oracle' gate can emit a false GREEN on the exact scenario it targets.
